message.content:
Here are some solid, beginner‑friendly brands to look at for a starter kit that includes both hand tools and power tools. Which one is best depends on your budget and how serious you want to get.

Budget‑friendly and reliable:
• Black+Decker – Great for light DIY, very inexpensive, common starter kits.  
• Ryobi – Best overall value for beginners. Huge range of tools on one battery system (18V ONE+). Good quality for the price.  
• Craftsman – Good balance of cost and durability, widely available, good mixed tool kits.

Mid‑range, higher durability:
• DeWalt – Very well‑built tools, long‑lasting batteries, excellent performance. Good for someone who wants to invest once and stick with it.  
• Makita – Smooth, reliable tools with excellent ergonomics. Slightly pricier but great for long‑term use.  
• Milwaukee – Strong, pro‑level brand; their M18 line is powerful and extensive. Often more expensive than needed for beginners but exceptionally durable.

Best choice for most people starting out:
• Ryobi (for best value and a huge ecosystem)  
• DeWalt (if you want to invest a bit more and keep tools long‑term)

If you tell me your budget and what type of projects you expect to tackle, I can recommend a specific kit.
